Inspector Information

This information will appear on all inspection records.

Include all digits. Do not include tenths. Example: if dash reads 142,500.7 enter 142500.

Section 2 — Vehicle & Body

Mark each item Pass, Fail, or N/A. Add notes and optional photo on any item.

Section 3 — Fluids & Frame

Inspect all fluid levels and check for leaks. Mark each item.

⚙️ Transmission Fluid — Electronic Check

Unit 123 uses an Allison transmission with electronic fluid level check. Follow these steps.

⚠️ IMPORTANT — Level Ground Required

The truck must be on completely level ground for an accurate transmission fluid reading. An uneven surface will give a false low reading.

  1. Park on level ground. Set the parking brake.
  2. Start the engine and allow the transmission to reach operating temperature (drive for several minutes if needed).
  3. Come to a complete stop. Place the selector in Neutral. Leave the engine running at idle.
  4. On the Allison shift selector pad, press the UP and DOWN arrow buttons simultaneously. A 2-minute countdown begins.
  5. The display shows *8 and counts down to 0 — one digit every 15 seconds. All conditions must be met or it will pause.
  6. After the countdown completes, read the display:
oLoK✅ Fluid level is correct
oLLo1⚠️ Fluid is 1 quart LOW — add fluid
oLHi1⚠️ Fluid is 1 quart HIGH — drain fluid
  1. To exit: press the Neutral button, or press UP + DOWN simultaneously twice.

☠️ CRUSH HAZARD — Read Before Proceeding

Inspecting under a raised dump box without the safety prop bars in place is a fatal hazard. The hydraulic system can fail without warning. Never go under a raised box without prop bars installed.

How to Raise the Dump Box & Set Safety Bars

Follow every step in order. Do not skip steps.

⚠️ Before You Start

Make sure the truck is on level, stable ground. Set the parking brake. Ensure no one is standing behind the truck. The dump box must be completely empty before raising for inspection.

Step-by-Step: Raise Box & Set Safety Props

  1. Engage the PTO (Power Take-Off) — located in the cab. The engine must be at idle before engaging PTO. Do not drive with PTO engaged.

    Cab controls (left to right): Allison shift selector (R/N/D + UP/DOWN for trans fluid check)  |  Parking brake (center lever)  |  Dump box hydraulic control (right lever)

  2. Slowly raise the dump box using the hydraulic lift control. Keep clear of the rear. Raise the box to its full upright position.
  3. Locate the safety prop bars — they are stored along the hoist/frame when not in use.

    📷 Note: When stowed, the prop bars fold down along the hoist frame. Locate them before you raise the box so you know where to reach once the box is up.

  4. Install both safety prop bars into their brackets/slots on the hoist cylinder. Both props must be seated before anyone approaches the rear of the truck.

    Both safety prop bars installed — one on each side of the hoist frame. The diagonal steel bars are locked into their brackets, mechanically supporting the raised box. Do not proceed under the box until BOTH bars are in place like this.

  5. Lower the box gently until it rests on the prop bars. The hydraulic system is now relieved and the box is mechanically supported.
  6. Now it is safe to inspect airlines, hydraulic hoses, and components above the rear axle that require the box to be raised.

    ⚠️ Note on the rear differential (back pumpkin): You do not need the box raised to check the pumpkin oil level — that can be done with the box down. Only raise the box when you need to inspect airlines and components that are otherwise blocked.

    The rear differential ("back pumpkin") is the large round dome housing. Check the fill plug (small round plug on the face) and inspect around the base for oil seepage. You do NOT need to raise the dump box to check the pumpkin oil level — only raise the box to inspect airlines, hoses, and components above it.

  7. When finished: Remove yourself and all tools from under the box. Raise the box slightly to free the props. Remove both prop bars and stow them. Lower the box completely. Disengage PTO.
